main.title: HWG
main.subtitle: Hexagonal Wargame Generator
main.lead: Design hex maps, build armies and test battle scenarios — from idea to a played match.
main.flow: Build a level in the Builder, then play it in the Playground.

levelBuilder.title: Level builder
levelBuilder.text: Assemble your own battle scenario step by step and export a ready-to-play level package.

features.map: Paint the hex map and its terrain
features.units: Place units, bases and spawn points

playground.title: Playground
playground.text: Load a level package and play a match live, commanding every unit yourself.

features.load: Load a built-in or custom level
features.play: Move, rotate and fire in real time

levelBuilder.title: Level builder
levelBuilder.subtitle: Build a level package step by step: map and deployment, units, turns & objectives, then review and export.

mapDeployment.title: Map & deployment
mapDeployment.hint: Set dimensions, generate terrain, paint cells, and mark spawn/base anchors.

units.title: Units
units.hint: Edit the player and enemy rosters that ship with the level package.

turntable.title: Turns & objectives
turntable.hint: Edit the per-side, per-phase D6 action matrix for both sides, then set the level victory conditions.

reviewExport.title: Review & export
reviewExport.hint: Validate the full level package and export it as a level archive.

automatedPlayground.title: Automated playground
automatedPlayground.subtitle: Replay a saved automated playtest trace.

mapCanvas.empty: Click "Generate map" to create a hexagonal map

unitsBuilder.introStart: Edit the rosters that ship with the level package. Deployment slots come from the spawn and base anchors placed in

unitRoster.fireAngleTitle: Fire angle: 0 fires straight ahead; 1 and 2 add that many hex directions to the left and right; 3 and 4 can fire in any direction.
unitRoster.empty: No units yet - add one below.

unitCatalog.title: Configure units
unitCatalog.name: Name
unitCatalog.nameTitle: Editable display name shown in rosters and unit labels.
unitCatalog.stableIdTitle: Stable unit type id used by rosters, exports, and imports.
unitCatalog.hpTitle: Health points: Units are destroyed when health reaches 0.
unitCatalog.actionsTitle: Actions: Movement points available during move actions.
unitCatalog.fireRangeTitle: Fire range: Maximum firing distance in hexes.
unitCatalog.fireAngleTitle: Fire angle: 0 fires straight ahead; 1 and 2 add that many hex directions to the left and right; 3 and 4 can fire in any direction.
unitCatalog.attackDamageTitle: Attack damage: Damage dealt by a successful attack.
unitCatalog.terrainPassabilityTitle: Terrain passability: Highest terrain difficulty this unit can enter.
unitCatalog.artilleryFireTitle: Artillery fire: On allows artillery-style fire over blocking terrain; off uses direct line of sight.
unitCatalog.iconTitle: Visual icon style used when rendering this unit type.
unitCatalog.unitTypeUsed: Unit type is used in a roster

unitsWorkflow.hint: Compare highlights matching unit types across player and enemy. Sync/lock copies player rows into enemy and keeps enemy read-only while player remains the editable source.

turntableBuilder.victoryNote: Choose one primary Team Blue objective. Team Red preview is generated from it.
turntableBuilder.baseUnavailable: Place at least one base to activate the base condition.
turntableBuilder.redBaseUnavailable: Place a Team Red base to use Occupy enemy base.
turntableBuilder.blueBaseUnavailable: Place a Team Blue base to use Protect own base.
turntableBuilder.redPreviewWipe: Red can always win by wiping Team Blue.
turntableBuilder.redPreviewStopOccupy: Red can also stop Blue by preventing occupation until the deadline, if a deadline is enabled.
turntableBuilder.redPreviewCaptureBlueBase: Red can win by occupying all Team Blue base hexes before or at the deadline.
turntableBuilder.redPreviewStopSurvive: Red must wipe Blue before the survive deadline.
turntableBuilder.playerActionOrderNote: Player action order is used only by automated scenario runs. During manual play, the player may choose any available action order.
turntableBuilder.emptyStart: No operations defined for this side. Use
turntableBuilder.emptyEnd: above to seed a validator-passing template.

automatedPlaytest.title: Automated playtest
automatedPlaytest.traceStorageUnavailable: Playback trace storage is unavailable in this browser.
automatedPlaytest.traceStorageQuotaExceeded: Playback trace could not be saved because browser session storage is full. Old automated traces were cleared; try running the match again with fewer max turns.

turntableWorkflow.hintStart: Edit dice cells below by toggling action chips. A face with no chips selected falls back to the no-op token
turntableWorkflow.hintMiddle: so the validator's "dice row must be an array" rule is preserved. Importing a
turntableWorkflow.hintEnd: file (or a full level archive) happens in

reviewExport.introStart: Validate the assembled level package, then export it as a single
reviewExport.introMiddle: (.zip). Section readiness below summarises what each step contributed - full validator output appears under
reviewExport.introEnd: Split JSON files remain available as a debug action.
reviewExport.hintLevelName: Level name is the editable base for the archive directory and file prefix; allowed characters are letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens. Export appends a timestamp suffix, for example
reviewExport.hintExportLevelEnd: directory with the four section files
reviewExport.hintTestInPlayground: sends the same timestamped level directly to Playground and keeps it available in the level dropdown there.
reviewExport.hintTestInAutomatedPlayground: sends the level to the Automated Playground where a configured AI runs one match automatically and records the trace.
reviewExport.hintExportSplit: downloads the same four files individually for hand-editing.
reviewExport.hintImportArchive: archive (all four section files, as produced by
reviewExport.hintImportMiddle: ) on its own, OR one to four split JSON section files, OR a single combined package JSON - but
reviewExport.hintImportMixed: mixed with JSON files (that would silently overlay archive sections). The merged candidate is validated before any state is replaced.
reviewExport.blocked: Export is blocked while errors are present above.

notifications.terrainSelectionRequired: Please select at least one terrain type
notifications.playtestHandoffFailed: Could not prepare the level handoff.
notifications.levelArchiveDownloaded: Archive "{levelId}.zip" downloaded. Unzip into public/ to load in Playground.
notifications.packageFilesDownloaded: {fileCount} files downloaded for "{levelId}". Drop them into public/{levelId}/ to load in Playground.
notifications.archiveImportAlone: A level archive (.zip) must be imported on its own. Select either a single .zip, or one or more JSON section files - not both.
notifications.unrecognizedJsonShape: unrecognized JSON shape (not a LevelPackage section)

draft.restoredBody: Your unsaved level draft was restored.

automatedPlayground.maxTurnsHint: Safety cap for the automated run — ends the match if the simulation drags on unexpectedly.
automatedPlayground.showRunLevelUnavailable: The level for this game is no longer available.
automatedPlayground.clearPlaybackTitle: Clear the current playback and free its session storage
automatedPlayground.levelNotReady: Selected level is not loaded yet.
automatedPlayground.traceStorageUnavailable: Playback trace storage is unavailable in this browser.
automatedPlayground.traceStorageQuotaExceeded: Playback trace could not be saved because browser session storage is full.
automatedPlayground.traceActionsHint: Export saves the current playback trace as JSON. Import loads a saved JSON trace into playback.
automatedPlayground.noBoard: No board snapshot in this frame.
automatedPlayground.boardSnapshotFallback: This frame has no board snapshot; showing the previous or level setup board state.
automatedPlayground.noDiceResult: No D6 result in this frame.
automatedPlayground.noAvailableActionsData: No available actions data for this frame.
automatedPlayground.noSelection: Select a hex on the playback map.
automatedPlayground.noUnitsForSide: No active units for this side.
automatedPlayground.resetGameResultsTitle: Clear game history and summary stats for a clean statistics run
automatedPlayground.noGames: Run a match to populate game history.
automatedPlayground.traceMissing: Trace was not found in session storage.
automatedPlayground.emptyInstruction: Run an automated playtest from Level Builder, step 4 Review & export. After the match finishes, open playback from the playtest panel.

splitters.engineMoves: Resize game engine and moves table panels
splitters.movesMap: Resize moves table and game map panels
splitters.mapSelection: Resize game map and selection panels
splitters.upperTimeline: Resize upper panels and game timeline

notifications.builderLevelExpiredBody: The playtest handoff could not be found. Select a saved builder level or load a ZIP.
notifications.levelLoadedReady: Level {levelId} loaded. Start game when ready.
notifications.levelRemovedBody: Removed {levelId} from the local Playground list.
notifications.archiveLoadedReady: Level {levelId} loaded from {sourceName}. Start game when ready.
notifications.archiveReadFailed: Could not read selected ZIP file.
notifications.restoreFailedFallback: Saved game could not be restored.
notifications.saveFailedFallback: Game state could not be saved.
notifications.resetConfirm: Reset the current game? The loaded level will stay ready to start again.
notifications.restartConfirm: Restart this game? Current progress will be cleared and the loaded level will start again.
notifications.gameProgressCleared: Game progress cleared. Loaded level is ready to start again.

moveForward.title: Move forward
moveForward.blocked: Cannot move forward: target hex is out of range, occupied, or terrain is too difficult.

reverse.title: Reverse
reverse.blocked: Cannot reverse: target hex is unreachable, or reverse is not allowed for current dice/phase/budget.

turn.title: Turn
turn.blocked: Cannot rotate: no actions remaining, or turn is not allowed for current dice/phase or budget.

fire.title: Fire
fire.noTargets: No valid targets in line of fire.
fire.missingTarget: Selected target entry is missing - try switching target.
fire.weaponNotLoaded: Weapon is not loaded. Reload before firing.
fire.blocked: Cannot fire: no actions left, dice does not allow fire in this phase, or other restrictions.
fire.invalidLineOfFire: Target is outside the valid attack zone (line of fire).

reload.title: Reload
reload.alreadyFull: Magazine is already full - nothing to reload.
reload.blocked: Cannot reload: no actions left, dice does not allow reload in this phase, or other restrictions.

inspector.fireTargetsInconsistent: Fire targets list is inconsistent (missing coordinates)

timeline.title: Game timeline
timeline.noRecordedActionsForTurn: No recorded actions for this turn
timeline.noActionsCurrentTurn: No actions recorded for the current turn
timeline.clickToUndoAction: Click to undo this action and every later action in this turn

notifications.snapshotExportDowngradedBody: This game cannot be exported as a v1 snapshot (likely a legacy save without RNG state). Exported history-only instead.

movesTable.noOperations: No operations defined for this side.
movesTable.multiSurfaceNote: Multiple surfaces are flattened into the operation column.
movesTable.noData: Moves data loads with the level. Initialize a game from the game engine panel.

map.title: Game map
map.noMapBody: Load a map from the map info panel to display it here

notifications.noTargets: No targets in line of fire.
notifications.lineOfFireFailed: Could not compute line of fire.

engine.title: Game engine
engine.rollDiceForActions: Roll dice to see available actions
engine.diceAlreadyRolledTitle: Dice already rolled this turn. End turn to roll again.

doctrine.title: Doctrine
doctrine.setupHint: Used only by the "auto-play turn" feature, so you can play against the bot instead of moving the opponent yourself.

autoPlay.title: Automatic turn play
autoPlay.hintNone: Automatic turn play is off for both sides. All actions on the board must be performed manually.
autoPlay.hintPlayer: Starting next turn, the Player's moves will be played automatically. You will only need to press the "End turn" button.
autoPlay.hintEnemy: Starting next turn, the Enemy's moves will be played automatically. You will only need to press the "End turn" button.
autoPlay.hintBoth: Starting next turn, the Player's and the Enemy's moves will be played automatically. You will only need to press the "End turn" button.
autoPlay.finishTurnPrefix: Finish the current turn of the
autoPlay.finishTurnCaptionPrefix: Automatically play out the current turn of the
autoPlay.finishTurnCaptionSuffix: using the chosen doctrine. Unlike "End turn", this spends the side's remaining actions instead of finishing in place.

notifications.playedBody: Auto-played {count} action(s) for {side}. Press "End turn" to continue.
